110083.explanation: Involvement and initiative from residents is extremely important! We prefer other means than a referendum, such as village councils, sounding boards, and participation processes tailored to and fitting current topics.

110087.explanation: Supporting start-ups and supporting the process.

110089.explanation: We face a national challenge; with the Distribution Act, we as a municipality have the duty to implement this. That does not mean an asylum seeker center must be established; that depends on many factors.

110092.explanation: This choice lies with the residents. Of course, nuisance to neighbors must be prevented. The municipality can inform residents about consciously reducing fires on poor air quality days and in calm weather.

110094.explanation: Facilitate, not organize. We gladly support residents’ own initiatives in all villages. It is not the municipality’s role to organize a fireworks show; we gladly support local initiatives, including financially.

110098.explanation: After completion and transformation, building within the existing building season is possible. The residential policy for rural areas already offers possibilities here. Building on fields in the countryside not adjacent to village cores is not possible; we wish to preserve our characteristic landscape.

110099.explanation: We find it important that everyone can participate in sports and/or culture. For this, we support associations/sports clubs so that fees can remain low and everyone can join. If this is not feasible or sufficient, the municipality provides financial support, for example via the Leergeld Foundation.

110105.explanation: It is more important to consider deployment; at what moments we deploy enforcement officers and what their focus should be. This can be done with the current staffing. If that proves insufficient, we will deploy more enforcement officers. Security is important.

110106.explanation: At specific moments, camera surveillance as currently done adds value; more camera surveillance is not necessary.

110108.explanation: CDA Bergeijk has requested to investigate the possibilities for this. Based on this research, this will be further developed; CDA Bergeijk is in favor.

110111.explanation: Holiday homes have as their primary function recreation.

110113.explanation: Everyone contributes according to their abilities. This way, everyone is part of society in their own way, involved and recognized.

110116.explanation: Loneliness among young people is a growing problem. We have a large community life in Bergeijk, but not everyone feels at home there and it's not enough for everyone. For them, youth work can provide a very important place where we continue to invest.

110117.explanation: The need is continuously monitored. If the demand increases, additional charging stations will be installed.

110118.explanation: Certainly in residential neighborhoods, but on some main roads 50 kilometers per hour remains the maximum.

110119.explanation: In the Netherlands, you have the right to choose your own healthcare provider (free choice of doctor). A point of attention is that general practitioners may have full practices and some health insurers impose restrictions. We maintain facilities; having enough general practitioners for sufficient choice is part of that.

110120.explanation: Keeping healthcare affordable is important, but quality comes first.

110123.explanation: Tourist tax is based on the turnover of entrepreneurs. If this increases, the tourist tax also increases.

110124.explanation: Preparations are currently underway to expand Waterlaat. We also want to facilitate local businesses in other villages. For large companies, we look at a large-scale business park in the Kempen region.

110128.explanation: We are examining how we can responsibly generate energy in Bergeijk. We await the process with residents to develop a new energy strategy based on that. Randomly placing solar panels on agricultural land in our municipality has no place in that strategy according to CDA Bergeijk.

110131.explanation: It is important that children receive swimming education. We wholeheartedly support financial assistance through, for example, Stichting Leergeld. We as a municipality do not financially support school swimming.

110132.explanation: The municipality is not responsible for the schools' curriculum. If they want to include this as part of the curriculum, we are happy to think along, but this is not a municipal task.

110133.explanation: A coffeeshop does not fit in a small municipality like Bergeijk. The establishment of a coffeeshop should therefore not be permitted by the municipality without reservation. As CDA Bergeijk, we prefer to focus on a (preventive) approach to counter normalization of drug use.

110139.explanation: Wind turbines are not an end in themselves for us. We await the process with residents to develop a new energy strategy based on that.

110140.explanation: Discussions with the province about the future of public transport are underway; more options like “call buses” will be available soon. If the discussions do not lead to the desired result, we will allocate additional funds if that leads to substantially better public transport.

110087.explanation: Sustainable energy generation is a municipal task. Energy saving is possible; subsidies are available for that.

110089.explanation: We searched with COA for a suitable location. Not found, so currently no asylum seekers' center (AZC) in Bergeijk.

110092.explanation: Few or no complaints. We need fewer rules, not more.

110094.explanation: Only under the strict condition that it can be done safely. And then in all villages.

110098.explanation: Municipal coordination in the interest of an attractive countryside. Greening, not concretizing.

110099.explanation: That's what Stichting Leergeld is for.

110105.explanation: A well-defined assignment must be established and monitored by the municipal council.

110108.explanation: We have been advocating this for years, so far in vain.

110111.explanation: We have two parks where that would be possible.

110116.explanation: On the contrary, more money is needed here.

110118.explanation: Where possible, that has already been done.

110119.explanation: Care has nothing to do with religion.

110123.explanation: We believe the tourist sector should contribute more to our green municipality.

110124.explanation: Examine very carefully given the spatial effects.

110128.explanation: Only very selectively and smaller than 5,000 square meters.

110131.explanation: A task for the national government.

110132.explanation: For primary education, we consider more physical education and music education more important. That is where LPB also wants to invest.

110133.explanation: Does not fit in a small village.

110139.explanation: Landscape pollution, bad for health and fauna.

110083.explanation: We strengthen local democracy by giving more people more influence.

110087.explanation: We involve all those citizens, companies, and organizations already working to shape the society of tomorrow. We want to work with energy communities that generate energy together and benefit together.

110089.explanation: We support the sheltering of people fleeing war and violence. An asylum seekers' center is possible but not always necessary. There are many ways to provide shelter to people.

110092.explanation: We notice that unfavorable weather causes nuisance regarding health and the environment. We always advise residents to consult the online 'Stookwijzer' (heating guide). We encourage alternative heating methods for the future.

110094.explanation: We want an alternative fireworks or light show organized by the municipality together with the local community.

110098.explanation: Agreements have been made about building in rural areas. The quality of the rural area is always central. There are many appropriate possibilities for building in the countryside.

110099.explanation: Children growing up in poverty receive a free membership of the sports club, the library, and cultural institutions.

110105.explanation: Pleasant neighborhoods and villages contribute to social safety. In a rapidly changing world, we want our villages to remain lively and further grow into places where you can grow up safely, live healthily, and actively participate in everything that makes life valuable.

110106.explanation: We value privacy more than control. In our villages, people address unwanted behavior with each other and solve it together.

110108.explanation: The focus in the coming years is on building social rental, mid-rental, and affordable owner-occupied homes for starters; therefore, it is important that young people can continue to use the starters' loan.

110111.explanation: As long as the housing crisis is not resolved, this remains an important option for many people to have somewhere to live. Think of people going through a divorce or other vulnerable groups.

110113.explanation: We will start working with basic jobs in our municipality. The core of such a basic job: you perform work for the community and receive an income to live on. We do not enforce this but see it as an opportunity for people receiving social assistance.

110116.explanation: We commit to more social workers who, together with village supporters, are the central connection in integration, youth participation, and prevention of drug and alcohol abuse.

110117.explanation: We continue investing in measures such as charging points for electric cars that contribute to the energy transition.

110118.explanation: A general 30 km/h speed limit is not necessary. Instead, we want car-reduced village centers and targeted investments in cycling infrastructure. This way, we increase traffic safety and livability without unnecessarily limiting accessibility.

110119.explanation: People must feel safe and understood in healthcare. Therefore, it is important that they can choose, where possible, a healthcare provider who aligns with their faith, culture, or beliefs. This contributes to good and dignified care.

110120.explanation: In youth care, quality must be leading. It concerns vulnerable children and families who have the right to good, appropriate, and reliable care. The lowest price should never be more important than people's well-being.

110123.explanation: Tourists use our facilities and add to the pressure on the municipality. A higher tourist tax ensures they also make a fair contribution.

110124.explanation: We support expanding business parks in Bergeijk if necessary to keep them economically healthy. This gives local companies space to continue operating here. For a large Kempish business park, Eersel is better suited due to its location and accessibility.

110128.explanation: We encourage solar parks on less fertile agricultural land as long as nature is enriched with, for example, herb- and flower-rich grasslands. This gives a boost to insects, birds, and small mammals.

110131.explanation: Swimming is a basic skill with which children can save themselves in life-threatening situations.

110132.explanation: This gives newcomers a better understanding of the local culture and helps them integrate more easily.

110133.explanation: A coffeeshop, according to national rules, can provide a safe place where soft drugs are sold. This reduces nuisance and allows people to receive education about responsible use, thereby better protecting health and safety.

110139.explanation: We are positive about a local energy community, for example with wind turbines, but only if residents benefit and can participate, and if nature and biodiversity genuinely improve.

110140.explanation: Good public transport is important, especially now our region is growing and people face traffic jams every day. The municipality must therefore invest more so that everyone can travel easily and affordably.

110083.explanation: VVD Bergeijk wants more participation from residents; therefore, for years we have supported a digital resident panel. This enables more residents to have a say with the municipality. A referendum for all important issues is probably too heavy a tool.

110087.explanation: Residents can receive a financial helping hand through subsidies.

110089.explanation: We comply with the Distribution Act (Spreidingswet).

110092.explanation: It is up to residents to use a wood stove, provided it does not cause nuisance to the neighborhood.

110094.explanation: From 2026, legal fireworks will no longer be allowed in the Netherlands. We consider finding a replacement logical.

110098.explanation: If this is possible from an urban planning perspective, VVD Bergeijk sees no problems with it.

110099.explanation: For children, this is different than for adults. Children should definitely have this option; for adults, it should be limited under conditions.

110105.explanation: There is a great need for control and enforcement. Fines are not always necessary, but initiating a conversation can be.

110106.explanation: There is camera surveillance at large events. However, VVD Bergeijk currently does not see the added value of permanent camera surveillance in public spaces.

110108.explanation: We need to develop this further. A starters' loan can provide that extra push, enabling more people to buy a home. But it can also lead to higher prices because people can pay more.

110111.explanation: Holiday homes may differ in quality from regular homes but are also subject to different rules.

110113.explanation: If someone receives benefits, that is tax money. It is only logical that we as a society expect something in return.

110116.explanation: We must be strict about these expenses. If it can be done differently or cheaper, then so much the better. If not, then it's not possible. Cutting youth work now can cause bigger problems in the future.

110117.explanation: This depends on the number of electric cars in our municipality.

110118.explanation: Of course, in residential areas. But main roads can remain 50 km/h if cycling can also be done safely there.

110119.explanation: We pursue this, but if it is not possible, then unfortunately it cannot be done.

110120.explanation: It is about the balance between quality and price. The most expensive is not necessarily the best, and the one with the highest quality does not have to be the most expensive.

110123.explanation: It is currently a percentage. If prices rise, the tax automatically increases. An additional increase is not necessary. Currently, €1.3 million is already collected annually from tourist tax.

110124.explanation: We fully agree with this. Small(er) businesses fit well within our municipality. Large companies should be able to locate at Kempisch Bedrijven Park 2.

110128.explanation: Solar panels belong on rooftops, not on (good) agricultural land that we can use for other purposes.

110131.explanation: This is not a government task. Things are going well now. If there is (large) demand for this, we can consider it.

110132.explanation: If there is a need for this, the municipality can facilitate it.

110133.explanation: As a border municipality, this is not desirable for us. We prefer to focus on more housing construction.

110139.explanation: VVD Bergeijk supports the generation of sustainable energy; it must fit with the green environment of Bergeijk. Wind turbines are not an end in themselves.

110140.explanation: If this leads to improved service provision.

statements.title: The municipality of Bergeijk should spend money on public transport in addition to the provincial budget.

titleParts.text: De gemeente Bergeijk moet geld uitgeven aan het openbaar vervoer, bovenop het

titleParts.glossary: A sum of money for a specific task or assignment.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Bergeijk.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to give attention to the Brabants dialect.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
Business parks in Bergeijk should be allowed to expand.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted in Bergeijk.

titleParts.text: In Bergeijk moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The residents of Bergeijk should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Bergeijk moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety should be employed in Bergeijk.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should cut spending on youth work.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
People and organizations that help young people in the municipality with various things.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: Wind turbines should be built in Bergeijk.

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.
